edge: Power Bundle Adjustment is introduced as an expansion type algorithm for solving large-scale bundle adjustment problems.
Power Bundle Adjustment is based on the power series expansion of the inverse Schur complement.
Power Bundle Adjustment constitutes a new family of solvers known as inverse expansion methods.
The approach of Power Bundle Adjustment is theoretically justified and its convergence is proved.
Using the real-world BAL dataset, it is shown that the proposed solver challenges state-of-the-art iterative methods.
The proposed Power Bundle Adjustment can complement a recently presented distributed bundle adjustment framework.

Large-scale bundle adjustment problems are typically solved by methods such as the conjugate gradient method, inexact Newton algorithms, and square root bundle adjustment. The choice of resolution method is influenced by the size of the normal equation.
Background information on power series is provided in the context of bundle adjustment methods.
The Schur complement is a prevalent method used to exploit the sparsity of the bundle adjustment problem.
Direct methods such as sparse and dense Cholesky factorization are compared to iterative methods like inexact Newton algorithms for solving large-scale bundle adjustment problems.
Square root bundle adjustment proposes a method to replace the Schur complement for eliminating landmarks, leading to significant performance improvements in bundle adjustment.
Although square root bundle adjustment is a performant solver for the bundle adjustment problem, it still relies on traditional solvers for the reduced camera system.
The PCG method is used for large-scale problems while Cholesky decomposition is used for small-scale problems in the context of solving the reduced camera system.
Solving the normal equation using PCG remains a bottleneck in finding thousands of unknown parameters and requires a large number of inner iterations.
Efforts have been made to improve the runtime of Bundle Adjustment (BA) with PCG by focusing on efficient parallelization, as mentioned in the context.
Stochastic BA was introduced to decompose the reduced camera system into subproblems and solve the smaller normal equation using dense factorization, as a way to improve the runtime.

The proposed method directly applies the power series expansion of the inverse Schur complement for solving the bundle adjustment problem, falling into the category of expansion methods. This method has never been applied to the bundle adjustment problem before.

Efficient storage is achieved by leveraging the special structure of the Bundle Adjustment (BA) problem and designing a memory-efficient storage method for the Jacobian matrices and residuals grouped by landmarks.

For a landmark with k observations, the pose Jacobian blocks, landmark Jacobian block, and residuals associated with the landmark are efficiently stored in memory blocks. Operations involved are parallelized using these memory blocks.

node_2: power expansion of the inverse schur complement
edge: A new class of large-scale bundle adjustment solvers is introduced that utilizes the power expansion of the inverse Schur complement.
Power series representation of the inverse Schur complement outperforms competitive iterative solvers in terms of speed, accuracy, and memory-consumption.
Power series representation can complement distributed bundle adjustment methods to boost performance for large-scale 3D reconstruction.
